questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 3 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times10\times10^{-6}}{3^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 2193.2$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 5 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times5^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=187.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The North-South and East-West Corridor are part of:
questions.solution: NS-EW corridors under NHDP Phase I/II.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The difference between pre-tensioning and post-tensioning is:
options: Pre-tensioning uses bars; Post uses wires
Pre-tensioning: steel tensioned before concreting; Post-tensioning: steel tensioned after
questions.solution: Timing of tensioning relative to concreting is the key difference.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 4 m carries a 8 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 8 kN, L = 4 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{8\times10^3\times4^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times8\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 106.7$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times4^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The right-of-way width for NH in open areas is:
questions.solution: ROW for NH = 30-60 m depending on terrain and category.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Magnel diagram is used for:
options: Determining the permissible zone of tendon eccentricity
questions.solution: Magnel diagram: graphical method to find eccentricity limits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 100 MPa, σy = 20 MPa and τxy = 40 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 100 MPa, σy = 20 MPa, τxy = 40 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=60$, radius $=56.57$; $\sigma_1=60+56.57$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=116.57$ MPa.

questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 4 m carries a UDL of 15 kN/m. The fixed-end moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IRC, the width of a single lane road is:
questions.solution: Single lane = 3.75 m; two-lane (without kerb) = 7.0 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In post-tensioning, the ducts are filled with grout to:
options: Protect steel from corrosion and provide bond
questions.solution: Grouting provides corrosion protection + bond (for bonded post-tensioning).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of the plastic moment to the yield moment for a rectangular section is:
questions.solution: Shape factor = M_p/M_y = S/Z = 1.5 for rectangle, 1.7 for circle, 1.14 for I-section.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In moment distribution, the process is continued until:
questions.solution: Iterate until carry-over moments are small (convergence criterion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On a two-lane highway, the stopping sight distance compared with the overtaking (passing) sight distance is:
questions.solution: Overtaking sight distance must cover the whole passing manoeuvre against opposing traffic, so it is several times the stopping sight distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The anchorage slip loss in post-tensioning is typically:
questions.solution: Anchorage slip = 5-8 mm depending on wedge type (IS 1343).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 8 m carries a UDL of 8 kN/m over its full length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = wL²/2 = 8×8²/2 = 256 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the method of tension coefficients, the force in a member is:
options: t × L where t = tension coefficient, L = length

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Superelevation is provided on:
questions.solution: Superelevation counters centrifugal force on horizontal curves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Type 1 prestressed concrete member has:
options: No permissible tensile stress under service loads
questions.solution: Type 1: no tension (full prestress). Type 2: limited tension. Type 3: no crack width limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 10 m carries a point load of 50 kN at the free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = PL = 50×10 = 500 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times4^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=120$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Marshall stability of a bituminous mix is a measure of:
options: Resistance to plastic flow under load
questions.solution: Marshall Stability = max load (kN) at failure in Marshall test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a post-tensioned beam, the tendon profile is usually:
questions.solution: Parabolic profile: max eccentricity at midspan, zero at supports.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a UDL of 5 kN/m. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The BM diagram of a simply supported beam under UDL is:
questions.solution: SSB + UDL: BM diagram = parabola (max at midspan).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In rigid pavements, the stresses are:
options: Maximum at the slab edges and corners
questions.solution: Critical stresses in rigid pavement: corner and edge (Westergaard).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Prestressing increases:
questions.solution: Prestress induces compression to offset tensile stresses from loads.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 100 mm wide and 300 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times4^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a water treatment plant, the chemical used for coagulation is typically:
questions.solution: Alum is the most common coagulant in water treatment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 3 m wide carries 12 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 12 m³/s, B = 3 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{12}{3}=4$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{4^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 1.18$ m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum daily water demand is typically:
questions.solution: Max daily = 1.5 × avg; max hourly = 1.5 × max daily = 2.25 × avg.

questions.stem: A beam-column is a member subjected to:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rigid pavements are generally made of:
questions.solution: Rigid pavements use a cement concrete slab that resists loads in flexure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The scale of a vertical photograph taken at 3000 m above terrain with f = 150 mm is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC beam has effective depth 500 mm, Ast = 1% of bd, fy = 415 MPa, fck = 25 MPa. Its moment capacity (limit state) is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 20 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 20 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{20\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=20$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel of width 3 m, depth 3 m, slope 0.002, Manning n = 0.015. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 5 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 0.8) under an initial effective stress of 80 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 5 m, e₀ = 0.8, σ₀ = 80 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times5000}{1+0.8}\log_{10}\tfrac{80+60}{80}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 270$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A notched bar under cyclic loading falls at stress below yield. This is called:
questions.solution: Fatigue = failure under repeated/reversed loading below yield strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentation tanks in water treatment primarily remove:
questions.solution: Sedimentation removes settleable suspended solids by gravity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The intermediate sight distance is equal to:
questions.solution: ISD = 2 × SSD (for overtaking on undivided roads with one-way traffic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trigonometric levelling involves measurement of:
questions.solution: Trigonometric levelling uses vertical angle and horizontal/vertical distance to compute DE.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The minimum reinforcement in a beam as per IS 456 is:
questions.solution: Ast_min = 0.85bd/fy for beams (0.12% for slabs, 0.15% for columns).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kinematic indeterminacy of a structure equals the number of:
questions.solution: Kinematic indeterminacy = number of independent unknown joint displacements (degrees of freedom).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As the depth of flow in a prismatic open channel approaches the critical depth at a fixed discharge, the specific energy:
questions.solution: For a given discharge the specific-energy curve has a minimum exactly at critical depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The dry unit weight of a soil is 16 kN/m³. If G = 2.65, the void ratio is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermal stress in a bar fully restrained at both ends is:
questions.solution: σ = EαΔT (independent of L). Strain = αΔT, stress = EαΔT.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The process of removing suspended solids by passing water through a porous medium is:
questions.solution: Filtration = physical removal of particles by porous media.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The minimum shoulder width on rural highways is:
questions.solution: Shoulder width = 2.5 m min (1.5 m in hilly terrain) as per IRC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principle of virtual work states that for a structure in equilibrium:
options: External virtual work = Internal virtual work

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.02, hydraulic radius R = 1.2 m and slope S = 0.0009, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.02, R = 1.2 m, S = 0.0009.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.02}\times1.2^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0009}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 1.69$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The relative precision of a traverse is expressed as:
questions.solution: Relative precision = closing error/perimeter = 1 in P/e.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The vapour pressure of water at 100°C is:
questions.solution: At boiling point, vapour pressure = atmospheric pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a chain survey, the tie line is used to:
options: Check the accuracy of the traverse
questions.solution: Tie lines = check lines to verify accuracy of traverse.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a pipe of diameter 0.1 m and length 6 m, water flows at 1.5 m/s. Darcy friction factor f = 0.03. The head loss due to friction is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The error due to the curvature of the earth in levelling is:
questions.solution: Earth's curvature makes distant points read lower; correction is +0.0785 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.015, hydraulic radius R = 0.5 m and slope S = 0.0009, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.015, R = 0.5 m, S = 0.0009.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.015}\times0.5^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0009}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 1.26$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 150 m. With back sight 1.5 m and fore sight 1 m, the RL of the next point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cable carrying a uniformly distributed load (horizontal projection) takes the shape of:
questions.solution: Cable under UDL (horizontal projection) = parabola. Under self-weight = catenary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a central point load of 20 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 20 kN, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{20\times6}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-jointed plane frame has 8 members and 5 joints. If it is simply supported, the structure is:
questions.solution: m = 2j - 3 for determinate truss. 8 = 2×5 - 3 = 7. Since 8 > 7, it's indeterminate by 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a single-bay, single-storey portal frame with both bases fixed is:
questions.solution: A fixed-base single-bay portal frame is indeterminate to the third degree.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 3 m² with velocity 2 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 2 m deep strip footing rests on sand (c = 0, φ = 35°). Using Nq = 33.3, the net ultimate bearing capacity per unit length is (γ = 16 kN/m³):
questions.solution: q_nu = γDN_q = 16×2×33.3 = 1065.6 kN/m² for strip footing (Terzaghi, c=0, ignoring Ng term).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 16 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 8 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a truss, a zero-force member may be identified when:
options: Two non-collinear members meet at a joint with no external load
questions.solution: If 2 non-collinear members at unloaded joint → both are zero-force members.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The continuity equation for incompressible steady flow in a pipe is:
questions.solution: Conservation of mass gives A1V1 = A2V2 for incompressible flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with an over-consolidated clay of the same type, a normally consolidated clay under the same load increment will:
questions.solution: A normally consolidated clay is more compressible (higher Cc on the virgin curve), so it settles more than an over-consolidated clay loaded below its preconsolidation pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 8 kN. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=60$, radius $=28.28$; $\sigma_1=60+28.28$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=88.28$ MPa.

questions.stem: The shear stress distribution across a rectangular beam section is:
questions.solution: Transverse shear stress varies parabolically, peaking at the neutral axis.

questions.stem: A thin-walled cylindrical pressure vessel of radius r and wall thickness t carries internal pressure p. The hoop stress is:
questions.solution: σ_h = pr/t for thin cylinders (hoop stress = circumferential stress).

questions.stem: A cantilever of span 8 m carries a point load of 10 kN at the free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = PL = 10×8 = 80 kNm at fixed end.

questions.solution: BM_max = wL²/2 = 8×3²/2 = 36 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The thermal stress developed in a fully restrained bar for a temperature rise ΔT is:
questions.solution: Restrained thermal stress = αEΔT, independent of length and area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slenderness ratio of a column is defined as:
questions.solution: Slenderness ratio = effective length / least radius of gyration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 2 m long carries a 4 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 4 × 2 = 8 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 20 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 20 kN/m, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{20\times6^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=90$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 3 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times3000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+60}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 122.5$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The lag distance in SSD is:
options: Distance travelled during reaction time (0.278 v t)
questions.solution: Lag distance = v × t (v in km/h) = 0.278 v t metres.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Influence lines are used to determine:
options: Variation of a function (BM, SF, reaction) as a unit load moves across the structure
questions.solution: IL = response function due to moving unit load.

questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 8 m carries a UDL of 5 kN/m. The fixed-end moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IRC, the desirable length of a transition curve is:
questions.solution: IRC empirical: L_s = 2.7 v²/R (v in km/h, R in m).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 6 m carries a UDL of 8 kN/m over its full length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = wL²/2 = 8×6²/2 = 144 kNm at fixed end.
